heading: Protections for domestic workers and other nonimmigrants
label: 1375b Protections for domestic workers and other nonimmigrants
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008, and not as part of the Immigration and Nationality Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Information pamphlet and video for consular waiting rooms
children.label: a Information pamphlet and video for consular waiting rooms

children.heading: Development and distribution
children.label: 1 Development and distribution
children.text: The Secretary of State, in consultation with the Secretary of Homeland Security, the Attorney General, and the Secretary of Labor, shall develop an information pamphlet and video on legal rights and resources for aliens applying for employment- or education-based nonimmigrant visas. The video shall be distributed and shown in consular waiting rooms in embassies and consulates appropriate to the circumstances that are determined to have the greatest concentration of employment or education-based non-immigrant visa applicants, and where sufficient video facilities exist in waiting or other rooms where applicants wait or convene. The Secretary of State is authorized to augment video facilities in such consulates or embassies in order to fulfill the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: In developing the information pamphlet under paragraph (1), the Secretary of State shall consult with nongovernmental organizations with expertise on the legal rights of workers and victims of severe forms of trafficking in persons.
the nonimmigrant visa application processes, including information about the portability of employment;
the legal rights of employment or education-based nonimmigrant visa holders under Federal immigration, labor, and employment law;
the illegality of slavery, peonage, trafficking in persons, sexual assault, extortion, blackmail, and worker exploitation in the United States;

children.label: A
children.text: the right of access to immigrant and labor rights groups;

children.label: B
children.text: the right to seek redress in United States courts;

children.label: C
children.text: the right to report abuse without retaliation;

children.label: D
children.text: the right of the nonimmigrant to relinquish possession of his or her passport to his or her employer;

children.label: E
children.text: the requirement of an employment contract between the employer and the nonimmigrant; and

children.label: F
children.text: an explanation of the rights and protections included in the contract described in subparagraph (E); and

children.label: A
children.text: anti-trafficking in persons telephone hotlines operated by the Federal Government;

children.label: B
children.text: the Operation Rescue and Restore hotline; and

children.label: C
children.text: a general description of the types of victims services available for individuals subject to trafficking in persons or worker exploitation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To best serve the language groups having the greatest concentration of employment-based nonimmigrant visas, the Secretary of State shall translate the information pamphlet and produce or dub the video developed under subsection (a) into all relevant foreign languages, to be determined by the Secretary based on the languages spoken by the greatest concentrations of employment- or education-based nonimmigrant visa applicants.

children.heading: Revision
children.label: 2 Revision
children.text: Every 2 years, the Secretary of State, in consultation with the Attorney General and the Secretary of Homeland Security, shall determine the specific languages into which the information pamphlet will be translated and the video produced or dubbed based on the languages spoken by the greatest concentrations of employment- or education-based nonimmigrant visa applicants.

children.heading: Posting on Federal websites
children.label: 1 Posting on Federal websites
children.text: The information pamphlet and video developed under subsection (a) shall be posted on the websites of the Department of State, the Department of Homeland Security, the Department of Justice, the Department of Labor, and all United States consular posts processing applications for employment- or education-based nonimmigrant visas.

children.label: A
children.text: government agency;

children.label: B
children.text: nongovernmental advocacy organization; or

children.label: C
children.text: foreign labor broker doing business in the United States.

children.heading: Deadline for pamphlet development and distribution
children.label: 3 Deadline for pamphlet development and distribution
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary of State shall distribute and make available the information pamphlet developed under subsection (a) in all the languages referred to in subsection (c).December 23, 2008

children.heading: Deadline for video development and distribution
children.label: 4 Deadline for video development and distribution
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary of State shall make available the video developed under subsection (a) produced or dubbed in all the languages referred to in subsection (c).March 7, 2013

children.heading: Responsibilities of consular officers of the Department of State
children.label: e Responsibilities of consular officers of the Department of State
i
children.text: confirm that the alien has received, read, and understood the contents of the pamphlet described in subsections (a) and (b); and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the alien has not received, read, or understood the contents of the pamphlet described in subsections (a) and (b), distribute and orally disclose to the alien the information described in paragraphs (2) and (3) in a language that the alien understands; and

children.label: B
children.text: offer to answer any questions the alien may have regarding the contents of the pamphlet described in subsections (a) and (b).

children.label: A
children.text: the legal rights of employment-based nonimmigrants under Federal immigration, labor, and employment laws;

children.label: B
children.text: the illegality of slavery, peonage, trafficking in persons, sexual assault, extortion, blackmail, and worker exploitation in the United States; and

children.label: i
children.text: the right of access to immigrant and labor rights groups;

children.label: ii
children.text: the right to seek redress in United States courts; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the right to report abuse without retaliation.

children.heading: Victim services
children.label: 3 Victim services
children.text: In carrying out the disclosure requirement under this subsection, the consular officer shall disclose to the alien the availability of services for victims of human trafficking and worker exploitation in the United States, including victim services complaint hotlines.

children.label: A
children.text: a nonimmigrant visa issued under subparagraph (A)(iii), (G)(v), (H), or (J) of ; andsection 1101(a)(15) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: any nonimmigrant visa issued to a personal or domestic servant who is accompanying or following to join an employer.

children.heading: Severe forms of trafficking in persons
children.label: 2 Severe forms of trafficking in persons
children.text: The term “severe forms of trafficking in persons” has the meaning given the term in .section 7102 of title 22

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 3 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of State.

children.heading: Abusing and exploiting
children.label: 4 Abusing and exploiting
children.text: The term “abusing and exploiting” means any conduct which would constitute a violation of section 1466A, 1589, 1591, 1592, 2251, or 2251A of title 18.
